name: Emain Ablach
description: Emain Ablach, also known as the Isle of Apples or the Isle of Women, is a mystical island in Celtic mythology, often associated with the Otherworld and the realm of the dead. It is described as a paradisiacal place, a land of eternal youth, beauty, and abundance, where sickness and death are unknown. The island is often shrouded in mist and located far to the west, accessible only through magical means or by those chosen by the gods. It is a place of healing and rejuvenation, where heroes and mythical figures journey to recover from wounds or seek wisdom. Emain Ablach is considered a sacred space, a bridge between the mortal world and the realm of the divine. It is associated with powerful goddesses and otherworldly beings, and is a symbol of hope, renewal, and the promise of eternal life. Some accounts describe it as a glass island, while others portray it as an orchard of magical apples that bestow immortality. The island's enigmatic nature and association with the Otherworld make it a central location in many Celtic tales and legends, representing a realm beyond human comprehension and a destination for those seeking ultimate knowledge and healing. Its connection to the sea and the setting sun further reinforces its symbolic association with death and rebirth.
subtitle: The Isle of Apples, Otherworld Paradise

keyMyths.title: Cú Chulainn's Healing
keyMyths.description: After being severely wounded, Cú Chulainn is brought to Emain Ablach to be healed by the goddesses of the island. He spends time there recovering his strength and learning from the inhabitants.

keyMyths.title: The Voyage to Emain Ablach
keyMyths.description: Various heroes and figures in Celtic mythology embark on journeys to find Emain Ablach, seeking its blessings and knowledge. These voyages often involve overcoming perilous trials and encounters with mythical creatures.

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of Celtic myths and figures.
sources.text: Comprehensive overview of Celtic myths and figures.
